mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below 96.5 signals supply-side weakness that constrains corporate profitability and earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment exceeding 200,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion that supports consumer income and spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 230.5) and elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 0.25%) signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predictive model.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts above 800,000 units annualized signals strong residential investment demand that supports construction employment and consumer wealth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in real GDP growth (GDPC1 declining quarter-over-quarter) combined with falling retail sales (RSAFS declining month-over-month) signals recessionary demand collapse that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines quarter-over-quarter AND RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient fails to reach statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
